questions.question: What does it mean for a function to be one-to-one?
options: A) The function is not injective
B) The function is injective but not surjective
C) Each element in the domain maps to a unique element in the codomain
D) The function is not injective and not surjective
questions.explanation: A function is one-to-one if each element in the domain maps to a unique element in the codomain.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a plain function?
questions.explanation: A plain function is one that has a unique output for every input.

questions.question: What is the domain of a one-to-one function?
options: A) The set of all possible inputs
B) The set of all possible outputs
C) The set of all real numbers
D) The set of all integers
questions.explanation: The domain of a function is the set of all possible inputs.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a one-to-one function?
questions.explanation: A function is one-to-one if each element in the domain maps to a unique element in the codomain.

questions.question: What is the range of a one-to-one function?
options: A) The set of all possible outputs
B) The set of all real numbers
C) The set of all integers
D) The set of all possible inputs
questions.explanation: The range of a function is the set of all possible outputs.
